page: Amazon Web Services i4i.12xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor operating at 3.5 GHz. The system includes 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage is a key feature, consisting of 11,250 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. The network interface provides a baseline bandwidth of 28 Gbps.

Benchmark results place the i4i.12xlarge in the average performance tier for most standard compute and memory workloads. The instance achieved a Geekbench multi-core score of 15,700.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 48,290.74. In specialized tests, it demonstrated strong performance in image processing with 14,996.0 on Geekbench and CPU encryption with 43,100.5 on Passmark. Memory bandwidth tests recorded 161.73 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 70.12 GB/sec for large block write operations. For database workloads, the server processed 44.86 million Redis SET operations per second and scored 20,855.81 in Passmark database operations.

While the instance excels in storage capacity and shows strong performance in encryption and image processing, it exhibits limitations in large-scale machine learning tasks. LLM inference testing with a 70B parameter model resulted in a weak performance tier, generating only 2.76 tokens per second. However, for smaller models, such as a 135M parameter model, it achieved a strong text generation speed of 298.89 tokens per second. Given the high local NVMe SSD capacity and balanced memory-to-core ratio, this server is designed for storage-intensive workloads, database operations, and data processing tasks that do not require GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services i4i.12xlarge is a storage-optimized server running on the Nitro hypervisor, equipped with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores from an Intel Xeon 8375C processor clocked at 3.5 GHz. It features 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a substantial 11,250 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, alongside a 28 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks indicate average tier results for general compute, with a Passmark CPU score of 48,290.74, but show strong capabilities in CPU encryption and image processing. Database operations are supported by a Redis SET throughput of 44.86 million operations per second. However, the lack of a GPU limits its utility for large-scale AI tasks, as demonstrated by a weak 2.76 tokens per second in 70B LLM text generation. This instance is best suited for large databases, storage-heavy applications, and data processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i4i.12xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and 11,250 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for database and storage-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i4i.12xlarge is a storage-optimized server with 48 Intel vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 11,250 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A storage-optimized x86_64 platform featuring massive local NVMe SSD capacity and balanced memory for database and data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores via Intel Xeon 8375C processor at 3.5 GHz
11,250 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-capacity local data access
384.0 GB of DDR4 memory with 28 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Strong performance in CPU encryption and image processing benchmarks
Suited for database operations, storage-heavy applications, and data processing
Weak performance on large-scale LLM inference due to lack of GPU acceleration
